steps.title: Spot the key contrast
steps.content: The treatment group exposed to harmless electric field shifts showed defensive responses lasting 71–87 seconds, while the control group resumed neutral behavior in less than one‑tenth of a second after the simulated attack.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to the choices
steps.content: Answer A states that some species of caterpillars can detect potential threats via changes in ambient electric fields, which directly explains why they defended longer only when exposed to those shifts. The other three either introduce details not mentioned in the passage (size of shifts, comparative sensitivity, causing shifts) or reverse the relationship. That matches choice A — A is correct.
